name: ORCA Framework Ecosystem Sweep
primary_research_question: What new host or framework capabilities now exist that ORCA Framework can exploit, wrap, adapt, or learn from?
integration_setup_watchlist: easier setup paths ORCA Framework could recommend
broken or deprecated setup patterns ORCA Framework should stop recommending
startup_stack_watchlist: Expo and React Native workflow changes
EAS build or update path changes
Supabase and Firebase mobile or web guidance changes
analytics and monitoring defaults for startup stacks
push, auth, and release-path changes that alter stack recommendations
notes: Research-first automation. No repo code or docs changes by default.
Reports should compare against the prior sweep and avoid repeating unchanged items as new.
Adopt-now findings should create one draft issue each unless a substantially similar issue is already tracked.
When a finding is promoted from watch or investigate-soon to adopt-now, preserve earlier sightings and backlink them from the draft issue.
Every material finding should explain whether ORCA Framework can use it directly, wrap it through a host adapter, document it as workflow guidance, or only watch it as an experimental pattern.
Scheduled runs must update the dated report, latest summary, living watch document, opportunities document, and any needed draft adopt issues.
Setup-path findings must explain how setup changed, who is affected, whether ORCA Framework guidance should change, and whether the recommendation is host-specific or portable.
Compatibility findings must update the matrix and harness watch, and should prefer unclear over unsupported when evidence is weak.
